corrections.description: File contains 'A Friend of the Helpless Dead' article instead of the expected 'A Bouquet and a Sentiment' article
corrections.text: A BOUQUET AND A SENTIMENT. Lula Greene Richards. This sweet, old-fashioned bouquet Of simple, quiet hue, Dear Sister Ann, because I can, I pluck today for you. Not for its costly raiment Nor for its fragrance rare, But that it draws for certain cause My thoughts with you to share. We've sung and worked in meetings, Have ranged our districts o'er, Have sowed good seeds and banished weeds For forty years and more. Your hands for work were suited, Mine never could excel, Yet willing each to learn or teach. We trust we've both done well. When differences I've settled, You've sometimes said my part Was oil to pour on feelings sore And soothe the wounded heart. Thus we have walked together With joyful piety; We love the aim, the noble name, "Relief Society." Life scenes are swiftly changing, Still, ever and anon. With work and song we'll help along The good cause, on and on. And may we all be thoughtful In this broad field of ours. To find and pluck and "for good luck" Take time to "scatter flowers."

corrections.text: For further information  address Mrs. Amy B. Lyman. General Secretary. 2R Bishop's Building, Salt Lake City. Utah.

notes: The extraction has several issues: 1) The first article file contains the wrong content (A Friend of the Helpless Dead instead of A Bouquet and a Sentiment), 2) Several files have content bleeding between articles, 3) Some Guide Lessons sections are incomplete or fragmented across multiple files, 4) Advertisement content appears mixed with article content in some files. The OCR quality appears good overall but article boundaries were not properly identified during extraction.
